summary: SCA's own reporting says the number of new school additions it delivers each year "fluctuates year-to-year based on the Five-Year Capital Plan," which scheduled an unusually large batch of projects to be completed in FY24 compared to the years before and after. The dip back to 4 additions in FY25 reflects that capital-plan phasing of when projects are finished, not a change in construction pace.

sources.label: NYC School Construction Authority, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing, though this reflects Five-Year Capital Plan phasing, not pace
